title: betula lenta var. papyrifera
intro: Betula lenta var. papyrifera, also known as paper birch, is a deciduous tree that is native to North America and is characterized by its distinctive white, papery bark. The medicinal properties of paper birch include its anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which have been used to treat a variety of health issues, including arthritis, skin conditions, and wounds. The health benefits of paper birch include its ability to reduce inflammation and pain, promote wound healing, and support the immune system. Paper birch can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and salves, and has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to treat a range of health conditions.
